text: Third / Second Conditional m If + Past Perfect, Would + Base Verb m To show how a past event that didn’t happen is causing a present effect. m * If 1 had studied medicine, | would be a doctor now. e If they had left on time, they wouldn't be stuck in traffic now. Second / Third Conditional m If + Past Simple, Would Have + Past Participle m To imagine how a different present state could have changed something in the past. w e If | were more organized, | would have finished the project earlier. e If he didn’t have a fear of flying, he would have joined us on the trip. @ vocaberry.com
